description.para: Standard MIPI CSI-2 data type identifiers for camera sensor interfaces

These constants define the data type field values used in MIPI CSI-2 packet headers to identify the format and encoding of transmitted image data. The data type field is 6 bits wide, allowing values from 0x00 to 0x3F.

briefdescription.para: NULL data type - used for padding or synchronization.
Blanking data - horizontal/vertical blanking information.
Embedded 8-bit data - sensor metadata or configuration data.
YUV 4:2:0 format with 8 bits per component.
YUV 4:2:0 format with 10 bits per component.
YUV 4:2:0 CSPS (Chroma Shifted Pixel Sampling) 8-bit format.
YUV 4:2:0 CSPS (Chroma Shifted Pixel Sampling) 10-bit format.
YUV 4:2:2 format with 8 bits per component.
YUV 4:2:2 format with 10 bits per component.
RGB format with 4 bits per color component.
RGB format with 5 bits per color component.
RGB format with 5-6-5 bits per R-G-B components.
RGB format with 6 bits per color component.
RGB format with 8 bits per color component.
Raw sensor data with 6 bits per pixel.
Raw sensor data with 7 bits per pixel.
Raw sensor data with 8 bits per pixel.
Raw sensor data with 10 bits per pixel.
Raw sensor data with 12 bits per pixel.
Raw sensor data with 14 bits per pixel.
User-defined data type generator macro.

detaileddescription.para: Generates user-defined data type identifier for custom or proprietary formats. The MIPI CSI-2 specification reserves data types 0x30 to 0x37 for user-specific implementations.

simplesect.para: Parameter n must be in range 0-7 to generate valid user-defined data types
Data type value in user-defined range (0x30-0x37)

briefdescription.para: input buffer type
output buffer type
video_buf_type enum

detaileddescription.para: Supported video buffer types of a video device. The direction (input or output) is defined from the device's point of view. Devices like cameras support only output type, encoders support only input types while m2m devices like ISP, PxP support both input and output types.

briefdescription.para: buffer is allocated from the internal video heap
buffer is allocated from outside
video_buf_memory enum

detaileddescription.para: Memory type of a buffer

briefdescription.para: discrete frame interval type
stepwise frame interval type
Supported frame interval type of a video device.
Buffer is done.
Buffer is aborted.
Buffer is in error.
Video signal result.

detaileddescription.para: Identify video event.

briefdescription.para: Current crop setting.
Crop bound (aka the maximum crop achievable)
Native size of the input frame.
Current compose setting.
Compose bound (aka the maximum compose achievable)
Video selection target.

detaileddescription.para: Used to indicate which selection to query or set on a video device

briefdescription.para: Set video format.

detaileddescription.para: Configure video device with a specific format.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to a video format struct.

briefdescription.para: Get video format.

detaileddescription.para: Get video device current video format.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Set video frame interval.

detaileddescription.para: Configure video device with a specific frame interval.
Drivers must not return an error solely because the requested interval doesn’t match the device capabilities. They must instead modify the interval to match what the hardware can provide.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to a video frame interval struct.

briefdescription.para: Get video frame interval.

detaileddescription.para: Get current frame interval of the video device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to a video frame interval struct.

briefdescription.para: List video frame intervals.

detaileddescription.para: List all supported video frame intervals of a given format.

Applications should fill the pixelformat, width and height fields of the video_frmival_enum struct first to form a query. Then, the index field is used to iterate through the supported frame intervals list.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to a video frame interval enumeration struct.

briefdescription.para: Enqueue a video buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Enqueue an empty (capturing) or filled (output) video buffer in the driver’s endpoint incoming queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct video_buffer **buf, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: video_dequeue

briefdescription.para: Dequeue a video buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Dequeue a filled (capturing) or displayed (output) buffer from the driver’s endpoint outgoing queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Flush endpoint buffers.

detaileddescription.para: A call to flush finishes when all endpoint buffers have been moved from incoming queue to outgoing queue. Either because canceled or fully processed through the video function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If true, cancel buffer processing instead of waiting for completion.

briefdescription.para: Start the video device function.

detaileddescription.para: video_stream_start is called to enter ‘streaming’ state (capture, output...). The driver may receive buffers with video_enqueue() before video_stream_start is called. If driver/device needs a minimum number of buffers before being able to start streaming, then driver set the min_vbuf_count to the related endpoint capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the buffers stream to start.

briefdescription.para: Stop the video device function.

detaileddescription.para: On video_stream_stop, driver must stop any transactions or wait until they finish.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the buffers stream to stop.

briefdescription.para: Get the capabilities of a video endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the video_caps struct to fill.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *const dev, const struct video_format_cap *const cap, struct video_format_cap *const res_cap, enum video_buf_type type, uint16_t ind)
memberdef.name: video_transform_cap

briefdescription.para: Transform a video format capability from one end to the other end of a m2m video device.

detaileddescription.para: This function transforms a video_format_cap from one end to the other end of an m2m video device. It allows applications to iteratively get all the supported capabilities on one end of the device given a single input capability on the other end.

Applications pass the addresses of a single input cap and a single res_cap together with the type of the res_cap and the index (started from 0) to iterate through to the function. The driver fills the res_cap structure and return 0 each time. Index should be incremented to get the next res_cap until the function returns an errno e.g., -EINVAL. For example:

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the source video format capability structure.
Pointer to the resulting video format capability structure, filled by the driver.
The video_buf_type of the resulting transformed cap.
Index of the resulting transformed cap.

briefdescription.para: Set the value of a control.

detaileddescription.para: This set the value of a video control, value type depends on control ID, and must be interpreted accordingly.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the video control struct.

briefdescription.para: Get the current value of a control.

detaileddescription.para: This retrieve the value of a video control, value type depends on control ID, and must be interpreted accordingly.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the video control struct.

briefdescription.para: Query information about a control.

detaileddescription.para: Applications set the id field of the query structure, the function fills the rest of this structure. It is possible to enumerate base class controls (i.e., VIDEO_CID_BASE + x) by calling this function with successive id values starting from VIDEO_CID_BASE up to and exclusive VIDEO_CID_LASTP1. The function may return -ENOTSUP if a control in this range is not supported. Applications can also enumerate private controls by starting at VIDEO_CID_PRIVATE_BASE and incrementing the id until the driver returns -ENOTSUP. For other control classes, it's a bit more difficult. Hence, the best way to enumerate all kinds of device's supported controls is to iterate with VIDEO_CTRL_FLAG_NEXT_CTRL.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the control query struct.
If the control id is invalid.
If the control id is not supported.

briefdescription.para: Print all the information of a control.

detaileddescription.para: Print all the information of a control including its name, type, flag, range, menu (if any) and current value, i.e. by invoking the video_get_ctrl(), in a human readble format.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the control query struct.

briefdescription.para: Register/Unregister k_poll signal for a video endpoint.

detaileddescription.para: Register a poll signal to the endpoint, which will be signaled on frame completion (done, aborted, error). Registering a NULL poll signal unregisters any previously registered signal.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Set video selection (crop/compose).

detaileddescription.para: Configure the optional crop and compose feature of a video device. Crop is first applied on the input frame, and the result of that crop is applied to the compose. The result of the compose (width/height) is equal to the format width/height given to the video_set_format function.
Some targets are inter-dependents. For instance, setting a VIDEO_SEL_TGT_CROP will reset VIDEO_SEL_TGT_COMPOSE to the same size.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to a video selection structure

briefdescription.para: Get video selection (crop/compose).

detaileddescription.para: Retrieve the current settings related to the crop and compose of the video device. This can also be used to read the native size of the input stream of the video device. This function can be used to read crop / compose capabilities of the device prior to performing configuration via the video_set_selection api.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to a video selection structure, type and target set by the caller

briefdescription.para: Allocate aligned video buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Size of the video buffer (in bytes).
Alignment of the requested memory, must be a power of two.

briefdescription.para: Allocate video buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Size of the video buffer (in bytes).

briefdescription.para: Release a video buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the video buffer to release.
on success or a negative errno on failure

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct video_format_cap *fmts, const struct video_format *fmt, size_t *idx)
memberdef.name: video_format_caps_index

briefdescription.para: Search for a format that matches in a list of capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: The format capability list to search.
The format to find in the list.
The pointer to a number of the first format that matches.

simplesect.para: -ENOENT when no matching format is found.

briefdescription.para: Compute the difference between two frame intervals.

parameterdescription.para: Frame interval to turn into microseconds.

simplesect.para: The frame interval value in microseconds.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct video_frmival_stepwise *stepwise, const struct video_frmival *desired, struct video_frmival *match)
memberdef.name: video_closest_frmival_stepwise

briefdescription.para: Find the closest match to a frame interval value within a stepwise frame interval.

parameterdescription.para: The stepwise frame interval range to search
The frame interval for which find the closest match
The resulting frame interval closest to desired

briefdescription.para: Find the closest match to a frame interval value within a video device.

detaileddescription.para: To compute the closest match, fill match with the following fields:

listitem.para: match->format to the video_format of interest.
match->discrete to the desired frame interval.

detaileddescription.para: The result will be loaded into match, with the following fields set:

listitem.para: match->discrete to the value of the closest frame interval.
match->index to the index of the closest frame interval.

parameterdescription.para: Frame interval enumerator with the query, and loaded with the result.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t bpp, uint8_t lane_nb)
memberdef.name: video_get_csi_link_freq

briefdescription.para: Return the link-frequency advertised by a device.

detaileddescription.para:
Device exposing a CSI link should advertise at least one of the following two controls:
VIDEO_CID_LINK_FREQ
VIDEO_CID_PIXEL_RATE

At first the helper will try read the VIDEO_CID_LINK_FREQ and if not available will approximate the link-frequency from the VIDEO_CID_PIXEL_RATE value, taking into consideration the bits per pixel of the format and the number of lanes.

parameterdescription.para: Amount of bits per pixel of the pixel format produced by the device

briefdescription.para: Estimate the size and pitch in bytes of a video_format.

detaileddescription.para: This helper should only be used by drivers that support the whole image frame.
For uncompressed formats, it gives the actual size and pitch of the whole raw image without any padding.
For compressed formats, it gives a rough estimate size of a complete compressed frame.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the video format structure

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative errno code

briefdescription.para: Set compose rectangle (if applicable) prior to setting format.

detaileddescription.para: Some devices expose compose capabilities, allowing them to apply a transformation (downscale / upscale) to the frame. For those devices, it is necessary to set the compose rectangle before being able to apply the frame format (which must have the same width / height as the compose rectangle width / height). In order to allow non-compose aware application to be able to control such devices, introduce a helper which, if available, will apply the compose rectangle prior to setting the format.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the video device struct to set format
Pointer to a video format struct.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *src, const struct device *sink, enum video_buf_type src_type, enum video_buf_type sink_type, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: video_transfer_buffer

briefdescription.para: Transfer a buffer between 2 video device.

detaileddescription.para: Helper function which dequeues a buffer from a source device and enqueues it into a sink device, changing its buffer type between the two.

parameterdescription.para: Video device from where buffer is dequeued (source)
Video device into which the buffer is queued (sink)
Video buffer type on the source device
Video buffer type on the sink device
Timeout to be applied on dequeue

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative errno code

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for video devices.
